title: Calvaria grandiflora
intro: Calvaria grandiflora, also known as the yellow bird's eye, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Africa and Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and small, white berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, thus offering benefits for cardiovascular health and immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Calvaria grandiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its pharmacological effects. In traditional medicine, Calvaria grandiflora is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used to make herbal supplements, tinctures, and teas.
taxonomy: Calvaria grandiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant belongs to the genus Calvaria, which is characterized by its showy flowers and woody stems. Calvaria grandiflora is also known by its synonym, Calvaria speciosa. The Ericaceae family, to which Calvaria grandiflora belongs, comprises a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as heath or heather.
